elements.name: Title
texts: The mountains and hills of Templecarn

elements.name: Description
texts: "Of mountains and hills there is a great number. The whole face of the country indeed consists of hills separated by narrow valleys..."

elements.name: Source
texts: Replies to Queries on Termon Magrath District from North West Farming Society, Parish of Templecarn, in Institute of Irish Studies, and Royal Irish Academy, Parishes of County Donegal / Edited by Angélique Day and Patrick McWilliams. II 1835-6, Mid, West and South Donegal., Ordnance Survey Memoirs of Ireland; v.39, p. 164

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Institute of Irish Studies in association with The Royal Irish Academy, Belfast

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "Of mountains and hills there is a great number. The whole face of the country indeed consists of hills separated by narrow valleys. These hills are unusually denominated from the townlands in which they are situated. The most considerable are Crocknacunny, Mindilpier, Croaghbrack and Rusheen. Overrun as they are with heath, they are pasturable to a certain degree, being covered in summer with considerable herds of black cattle. Cultivation had during the last war crept up their sides to a good height but, through the low prices of agricultural products and the bad prospect of remuneration, has since reverted again to the vales."
